steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.evangelizare: 'evangelizare' is an infinitive (to preach), not a finite verb

steps.target: to be necessary / it is fitting
choices: to be necessary / it is fitting

hints.to open: 'to open' is 'aperire' — 'Oportet' comes from 'oportere' (to be necessary)
hints.to hope: 'to hope' is 'sperare' — 'Oportet' comes from 'oportere' (it is necessary)

hints.aperio, aperire, aperui, apertum: These are principal parts of 'aperio' (to open)
hints.operor, operari, operatus sum, —: These are principal parts of 'operor' (to work)
hints.oppono, opponere, opposui, oppositum: These are principal parts of 'oppono' (to oppose)

hints.imperative: Imperative would be 'evangeliza' — 'evangelizare' is the infinitive
hints.participle: Participle would be 'evangelizans' — 'evangelizare' is the infinitive
hints.gerund: Gerund would be 'evangelizandi' — 'evangelizare' is the present active infinitive

hints.nuntio, nuntiare, nuntiavi, nuntiatum: These are principal parts of 'nuntio' (to announce)
hints.praedico, praedicare, praedicavi, praedi: These are principal parts of 'praedico' (to proclaim)
hints.baptizo, baptizare, baptizavi, baptizatu: These are principal parts of 'baptizo' (to baptize)

hints.nominative: Nominative neuter 'regnum' has the same form, but here it is the object of 'evangelizare'
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'regno' — 'regnum' is accusative, direct object

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'regni' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'regni' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'regni' ends in -i

hints.nominative: Nominative is 'Deus' — 'Dei' is genitive (the kingdom OF God)
hints.dative: Dative would be 'Deo' — 'Dei' is genitive singular

translation: I must preach the kingdom of God
